selected_issue.issue: canonical test execution fails in clean bootstrap contexts because dev test dependencies are not installed
selected_issue.title: scripts/bootstrap.ps1 installs requirements.txt only, so pytest is missing for canonical test runs

why_it_outranked_alternatives: Current evidence showed no rank-1 structural invariant violations and no rank-3/4 replay mismatch or high-frequency diagnostic failures. A rank-2 issue was reproducible at baseline: python3 -m pytest -q failed with 'No module named pytest', so this issue outranked lower-tier candidates.

checks.result: failed pre-bootstrap: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'fastapi'
bootstrap installed requirements.txt via pip install -r $ReqTxt and did not install requirements-dev.txt

files_changed.change: Added requirements-dev.txt existence guard and switched install target to requirements-dev.txt.

before_after_evidence.before: Canonical tests failed in clean run context with missing pytest, replay assessment was blocked by missing fastapi, and bootstrap installed runtime requirements only.
before_after_evidence.after: Bootstrap now installs requirements-dev.txt (including test dependencies), and canonical pytest plus replay assessment execute successfully in this run.

unresolved_risks: PowerShell bootstrap script execution was not directly run in this Linux sandbox due missing pwsh.
requirements-dev.txt keeps pytest and pytest-asyncio unpinned, allowing potential future drift.
stop_reason: Selected rank-2 issue fixed with minimal bounded changes and validated; additional work would exceed single-issue scope.
